zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew於1358年出生在威爾士格拉莫爾根澤德時，他的父親Sir Gruffydd Gethyn ap Madog Lord of Cardigan33歲，母親Mallt verch Rhun37歲。他和Mrs. Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew至少有1個女兒。

zh-hans.Senghenydd, Gwlad Morgan, Wales: 威尔士格拉莫爾根澤德
zh-hans.of Llwyn Morgan,Llandeilo Fawr,Carmarthe: 威尔士卡馬特史爾利安代洛
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew于1358年出生在威尔士格拉莫爾根澤德时，他的父亲Sir Gruffydd Gethyn ap Madog Lord of Cardigan33岁，母亲Mallt verch Rhun37岁。他和Mrs. Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: Mathew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Mathai, Babu, Oommen, Saji, Varughese, Raju, Reji, Sunil, Prakash, Prasad, Shaji, Cheriyan.
English and Scottish: variant of Matthew . Mathew is common as a personal name among Christians in South India, and was registered as a surname after their immigration to the US.

variants.name: Mathews
meanings: English: patronymic from   Mathew  ; a variant of   Matthews  . In North America, this surname has absorbed various cognates from other languages.

History: Among the earliest bearers of the name in North America was Samuel Mathews (c. 1600–c. 1657), who came to VA from London c. 1618. He established a plantation at the mouth of the Warwick River, which was at first called Mathews Manor; later its name was changed to Denbigh. He was one of the most powerful and influential men in the early affairs of the colony. He (or possibly his son, who bore the same name) was governor of the colony from 1657 until his death in 1660.

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

nameMeanings.name: Knight
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

nameMeanings.name: Leuan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Iohannes. See John .

variants.name: Iefan
meanings: Later form of   Ieuan .

variants.name: Ifan
meanings: From Latin Iohannes. See John .

variants.name: John
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: Gruffudd
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Welsh Grip(p)iud, from an unexplained first element + a second element meaning ‘lord, prince’. Gruffydd ap Llewelyn ( d. 1063 ) was one of the most able rulers of Wales in the Middle Ages, scoring notable victories over the English until he was eventually defeated by King Harold in 1063 .

variants.name: Griffudd
meanings: From Old Welsh Grip(p)iud, from an unexplained first element + a second element meaning ‘lord, prince’. Gruffydd ap Llewelyn ( d. 1063 ) was one of the most able rulers of Wales in the Middle Ages, scoring notable victories over the English until he was eventually defeated by King Harold in 1063 .

variants.name: Gruffud
meanings: From Old Welsh Grip(p)iud, from an unexplained first element + a second element meaning ‘lord, prince’. Gruffydd ap Llewelyn ( d. 1063 ) was one of the most able rulers of Wales in the Middle Ages, scoring notable victories over the English until he was eventually defeated by King Harold in 1063 .

variants.name: Griffith
meanings: Anglicized form of   Gruffudd .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew was born in 1358, in Senghenydd, Glamorgan, Wales, his father, Sir Gruffydd Gethyn ap Madog Lord of Cardigan, was 33 and his mother, Mallt verch Rhun, was 37. He had at least 1 daughter with Mrs. Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew est né en 1358, à Senghenydd, Glamorgan, Galles, son père, Sir Gruffydd Gethyn ap Madog Lord of Cardigan, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Mallt verch Rhun, avait 37 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Mrs. Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew 1358 in Senghenydd, Glamorgan, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Gruffydd Gethyn ap Madog Lord of Cardigan 33 und seine Mutter Mallt verch Rhun 37. Er hatte mit Mrs. Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew è nato il nel 1358 nel Senghenydd, Glamorgan, Galles, il suo padre, Sir Gruffydd Gethyn ap Madog Lord of Cardigan, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Mallt verch Rhun, aveva 37 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Mrs. Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathewが1358年にウェールズグラモーガンセングヘニーッドで生まれた時，父Sir Gruffydd Gethyn ap Madog Lord of Cardiganは33歳，母Mallt verch Rhunは37歳であった。彼はMrs. Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathewとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew(이)가 1358년 웨일스 글램르건 센게이니드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Gruffydd Gethyn ap Madog Lord of Cardigan(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Mallt verch Rhun(은)는 37세였다. 그는 Mrs. Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew nasceu em 1358, em Senghenydd, Glamorgan, Gales, seu pai, Sir Gruffydd Gethyn ap Madog Lord of Cardigan, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Mallt verch Rhun, tinha 37 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Mrs. Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew родился в 1358 г. в местности сеньхенидд, глэморган, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Sir Gruffydd Gethyn ap Madog Lord of Cardigan было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Mallt verch Rhun, 37. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Mrs. Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew nació en 1358, en Senghenydd, Glamorgan, Gales, su papá, Sir Gruffydd Gethyn ap Madog Lord of Cardigan, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Mallt verch Rhun, tenía 37 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Mrs. Sir Knight Leuan ap Gruffudd Mathew.
